content: Symptom management principles. Pain: WHO analgesic ladder (non-opioids → weak opioids → strong opioids); adjuvant analgesics (gabapentin/pregabalin for neuropathic pain, antidepressants, corticosteroids for bone pain/capsular pain); breakthrough dosing 10-15% of total daily opioid; bowel regimen mandatory with opioids; rotate opioids for inadequate response or side effects. Dyspnea: opioids reduce air hunger; supplemental oxygen if hypoxic; fans/cool air; benzodiazepines for anxiety component; thoracentesis for malignant effusions. Nausea/vomiting: antiemetics by mechanism (5-HT3 antagonists, dopamine antagonists, NK1 antagonists, antihistamines); treat underlying cause (constipation, obstruction, metabolic abnormalities, medications). Fatigue: energy conservation, graded exercise, treat reversible causes (anemia, hypothyroidism, depression); psychostimulants sometimes used. Anorexia/cachexia: small frequent meals, appetite stimulants (megestrol, steroids), address reversible factors. Communication: SPIKES protocol for breaking bad news; REMAP for goals of care discussions; discuss prognosis honestly but with hope; involve family as patient desires.
